title: NIS2 Supervisory Compliance Evidence Pack Readiness

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.32 is assigned contributing assurance because this UC provides concrete Splunk evidence for the observable control condition, while policy approval, legal interpretation, and official filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.32 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.35: NIS2 Supervisory Compliance Evidence Pack Readiness.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.35' running on sourcetype scheduler and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.33 is assigned contributing assurance because this UC provides concrete Splunk evidence for the observable control condition, while policy approval, legal interpretation, and official filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.33 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.35: NIS2 Supervisory Compliance Evidence Pack Readiness.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.35' running on sourcetype scheduler and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=_audit` and `index=_internal` with `sourcetype=scheduler` for skip/fail telemetry; `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:audit_finding` (fields `finding_id`, `status`, `owner`, `due_date`); `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` for policy/evidence tasks; optional export landing checks in `index=audit_evidence`. Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) supplies normalized ServiceNow fields when ITSM/GRC tables are ingested.
app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform; Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) for `snow:sn_grc_policy`-style feeds.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_evidence_report_catalog.csv
| join savedsearch_name type=left [
    search index=_audit action=search earliest=-7d
    | stats latest(total_run_time) as last_runtime latest(info) as result by savedsearch_name
]
| where isnull(last_runtime) OR match(result,"(?i)fail|error|skipped")
| lookup nis2_evidence_kfp_exceptions savedsearch_name OUTPUT exception_until
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table savedsearch_name, owner, last_runtime, result
description: Flags scheduled Splunk saved searches that contribute to supervisory evidence packs when they did not run in the last week or finished with failure, skip, or error — the operational heartbeat for automated compliance artefacts before a regulator or internal audit requests them.
value: Directors and operational risk teams get an early warning when the automation that produces inspection-ready material has broken, so legal and GRC can intervene before a deadline rather than explaining missing attachments after the fact.
implementation: (1) Maintain `nis2_evidence_report_catalog.csv` with one row per evidence job (`savedsearch_name`, `owner`, product scope). (2) Ingest GRC/task correlation via Splunkbase 1928 if ServiceNow owns evidence workflows. (3) Schedule this search daily with a 7-day window. (4) Route alerts to the named owner and archive pass/fail snapshots to `index=audit_evidence`. (5) Optional: mirror PDF/CSV outputs with a scripted output that writes to WORM — see `outputs.conf` on the search head.
visualization: Table of failing or stale jobs keyed on `savedsearch_name`, single value count of open gaps, timeline of `last_runtime` by owner.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Monitor scheduler health (internal indexes)

knownFalsePositives:
• **ServiceNow GRC rename drift** — Distinguish: `_audit` shows success but `grc:audit_finding` still references the old job label. Suppress: update `nis2_evidence_report_catalog.csv` under change control; use `lookup nis2_evidence_kfp_exceptions` with a 14-day time-bound exception for cutover.
• **Planned indexer or search-head maintenance** — Distinguish: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=scheduler` shows skips tied to known hosts in a maintenance roster. Suppress: join a `maintenance_windows` lookup on `splunk_server` and add `where in_window=0` to the alert SPL.
• **Legal hold pausing reports** — Distinguish: GRC ticket documents intentional pause. Suppress: maintain an exception register column `legal_hold` on the catalogue; filter with `where legal_hold!="true"` until counsel closes the hold.
• **Intentional throttle during a major incident** — Distinguish: incident commander record in ServiceNow points at disaster search head queue. Suppress: time-bound exception row in `nis2_evidence_kfp_exceptions.csv` reviewed every 48 hours.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud roles: readers need `srchIndexesAllowed` covering `_audit`, `_internal`, `grc`, `itsm`, and `audit_evidence` where used.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) on forwarders/search tier if `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` events are required for human workflow context.
• CSV lookup `nis2_evidence_report_catalog.csv` in `lookups/` with fields at least `savedsearch_name`, `owner`; optional `legal_hold` for filtering.
• Optional `nis2_evidence_kfp_exceptions.csv` with `savedsearch_name`, `exception_until` for time-bound suppressions (review every 7 days).
• Clock skew under 60 seconds between search heads and indexers — otherwise `_audit` correlation lags `scheduler` events.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Index internal scheduler diagnostics: confirm `index=_internal` `sourcetype=scheduler` is retained ≥30 days on indexers handling saved-search coordination. Enable `_audit` trail for saved search executions (`action=search`). For ServiceNow-backed evidence tasks, enable the modular inputs or HEC feeds from the Splunkbase 1928 package so `index=itsm` receives `snow:sn_grc_policy` with extracted fields like `number`, `state`, `short_description`. Map `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:audit_finding` with `finding_id`, `status`, `owner` if legal ties findings to specific jobs. Document the API/table path (for example REST `/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy`) in your integration guide so auditors can trace lineage.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run interactively, then save as alert `UC-22.2.35` on a daily cron (recommended 06:00 local) over `earliest=-7d`.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_evidence_report_catalog.csv
| join savedsearch_name type=left [
    search index=_audit action=search earliest=-7d
    | stats latest(total_run_time) as last_runtime latest(info) as result by savedsearch_name
]
| where isnull(last_runtime) OR match(result,"(?i)fail|error|skipped")
| lookup nis2_evidence_kfp_exceptions savedsearch_name OUTPUT exception_until
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table savedsearch_name, owner, last_runtime, result
```

Understanding this SPL: the lookup lists every pack-critical job. The subsearch pulls latest `_audit` outcomes; null `last_runtime` means no successful search logged in-window. Post-filter removes approved exceptions. Throttle alerts by `savedsearch_name` for 24 hours to avoid noise during indexer restart storms.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick three catalogue rows. For each, run `index=_audit action=search savedsearch_name="<name>" earliest=-7d` and confirm `info` matches the panel. Cross-check `index=_internal` `sourcetype=scheduler` for skip reasons (`status` fields). If ServiceNow is in scope, open the same ticket IDs via the UI and compare state to `snow:sn_grc_policy` events. Spot-check that PDF exports (if any) landed in `index=audit_evidence` with matching `_time` buckets.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add a dashboard row: table of failing jobs, drilldown to Job Inspector (`Settings` > `Searches, reports, and alerts`). Wire alert actions to email plus webhook to the GRC queue. Archive each alert firing to `index=audit_evidence` using a summary indexing stream or export job. Document the runbook path in Confluence/SharePoint and assign a secondary owner for holiday coverage.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Empty `_audit` results** — search head audit trail disabled; enable in `audit.conf` / `Server Settings` > `Audit Logging`. RBAC must allow `_audit` reads.
• **Everything shows success but pack files stale** — downstream `outputs.conf` action failing; inspect `$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/python.log` for scripted output traceback.
• **Join explosion** — duplicate rows in catalogue; enforce unique `savedsearch_name` in the lookup editor (`Settings` > `Lookups`).
• **Persistent false positives after DR failover** — add 72-hour time-bound exceptions via `nis2_evidence_kfp_exceptions.csv` until DNS for deployment server stabilises.
grandmaExplanation: We check whether the scheduled jobs that build your regulator-ready paperwork actually ran on time. If a job failed or never finished, we raise a hand so the right owner can fix it before anyone official asks for the files.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel or competent-authority review; does not attest to the legal sufficiency of pack contents.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.35`, dashboard drilldown to `_audit` events, optional `index=audit_evidence` export, and time-aligned `grc:audit_finding` records for supervisory traceability.

controlTest.positiveScenario: For a catalogued job deliberately disabled for one business day, `_audit` lacks a successful `action=search` row within the 7-day window and the alert returns that `savedsearch_name`, empty `last_runtime`, and a null or failed `result` plus the configured owner from the lookup.
controlTest.negativeScenario: For a catalogued job that completed successfully each day, the joining `_audit` stats show a fresh `last_runtime` within SLA and `result=success`, so the row is excluded after the post-filter even when neighbouring jobs in the same app fail.
